item.title: Gunman opens fire on Memorial Drive in Cambridge, 2 injured
item.description: A gunman who opened fire at cars on Memorial Drive in Cambridge, Massachusetts Monday afternoon was shot by a responding State Police trooper and a civilian.

item.title: Iran vows to fight on as Trump says ceasefire is "on life support"
item.description: President Trump is expected to encourage China to pressure Iran into making a deal to end the war when he visits Beijing later this week and meets with President Xi Jinping.

item.title: Trump says he aims to suspend gas tax "for a period of time"
item.description: President Trump made the comments in a phone interview with CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes.

item.title: Trump proposes gas tax holiday, but savings may be limited
item.description: Suspending the federal gas tax would have a modest impact on fuel prices, while also requiring congressional approval.

item.title: School principal who was shot tackling gunman: "It was just instinct"
item.description: Kirk Moore, an Oklahoma high school principal, took a bullet tackling a gunman in his school's lobby. He told CBS News what he did was "just instinct" and said he didn't even realize he'd been shot at first. Matt Gutman has more.

item.title: Netanyahu discusses China's support for Iran
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu spoke with CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett about the situation in Iran. Ahead of President Trump's meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping, Netanyahu said he "didn't like" that China supported Iran's war efforts.

item.title: Trump wants to suspend gas tax amid high prices due to war with Iran
item.description: President Trump told CBS News' Nancy Cordes that he wants to temporarily lift the federal gas tax as prices continue to rise amid the ongoing war with Iran. Mr. Trump also said the latest response from Iran to end the war was "a bad proposal ... done by people that have no clue as to the danger they're in."

item.title: The China-Taiwan-U.S. relationship balancing act
item.description: President Trump told reporters on Monday he expects to speak with Chinese President Xi Jinping about the U.S.' relationship with Taiwan, specifically its longstanding policy of selling weapons to the island. CBS News' Lindsey Reiser breaks down the delicate balancing act.

item.title: NIH director on hantavirus risk
item.description: Dr. Jay Bhattacharya, director of the National Institutes of Health and acting director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, spoke with "CBS Evening News" anchor Tony Dokoupil about what risks hantavirus poses to the U.S. public.

item.title: Retired Navy vice admiral says best way to remove Iran's enriched uranium is to "negotiate a deal"
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told CBS News that the war with Iran won't end until the nation's enriched uranium is removed. Ret. Navy Vice Adm. Robert Murrett joins with his perspective.

item.title: 6 found dead in shipping container at rail yard near Texas border
item.description: Police confirmed that there were six people dead, five men and one woman.

item.title: Texas sheriff says 7th body could be tied to shipping container deaths
item.description: The body of a seventh person was located Monday nearly 150 miles north of a Union Pacific rail yard in Laredo, where six bodies were discovered on Sunday afternoon.

item.title: Why hantavirus incubation period worries officials
item.description: Seventeen Americans and a dual U.S.-British citizen are back in the U.S. after being evacuated from the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak. CBS News chief medical correspondent Dr. Jon LaPook has the latest.

item.title: Would a federal gas tax pause give Americans financial relief?
item.description: President Trump told CBS News on Monday that he wants to suspend the federal fuel tax as the war with Iran continues to send gas prices skyrocketing. CBS News business contributor Javier David joins with analysis.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: The Gulf War
item.description: CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe introduces CBS News Radio's coverage of the beginning of the Gulf War against Iraq and 1991's Operation Desert Storm.

item.title: Supreme Court clears path for Alabama to redraw congressional map
item.description: The Supreme Court set aside lower court decisions that had blocked the state from using a congressional map drawn by Republicans in 2023 that contained one majority-Black district.

item.title: CDC leader says hantavirus not "a five-alarm fire bell"
item.description: Jay Bhattacharya, the acting director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, told CBS News that the hantavirus outbreak should be treated differently from COVID.

item.title: 5/11: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: President Trump says ceasefire with Iran "on life support"; Americans exposed to hantavirus return to quarantine in the U.S.

item.title: Elon Musk and other CEOs invited to be in Trump's delegation to China
item.description: Elon Musk, Tim Cook and other high-powered business leaders have been invited to be a part of the U.S. delegation traveling to China this week.

item.title: Supreme Court continues to temporarily maintain mail access to abortion pill
item.description: Justice Samuel Alito extended an administrative stay that maintained access to mifepristone through the mail.

item.title: White House ballroom funds hang over GOP push to fund ICE
item.description: The Senate is returning to Washington to resume work on funding immigration agencies with a package that includes $1 billion for the renovation of the White House East Wing.

item.title: Cartel figure detained as officials recover drugs, guns and 7 tigers
item.description: Authorities said they seized unidentified narcotics, cash, 10 guns, 11 vehicles, six motorcycles — and seven tigers.

item.title: Years after sex abuse scandal, a young gymnast says "it happened again"
item.description: Sean Gardner, a gymnastics coach who trained elite young girls, will be in federal court in Mississippi on Monday facing 12 felony counts of sexual exploitation of children.

item.title: Why Americans from the hantavirus-stricken ship were taken to Omaha
item.description: Most of the Americans who were on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak were taken to specialized facilities at the University of Nebraska Medical Center.

item.title: Trump approves plan to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary
item.description: Marty Makary has served as Food and Drug Administration commissioner since March 2025.

item.title: Coast Guard seizes sailboat in Lynette Hooker's disappearance probe, sources say
item.description: The sailboat used by Brian and Lynette Hooker​ in their travels around the Bahamas — named "Soulmate" — has been seized by U.S. Coast Guard investigators.

item.title: Americans from hantavirus-hit ship arrive in U.S., including 1 who tested positive
item.description: An American on the repatriation flight began showing symptoms of hantavirus and another "tested mildly PCR positive for the Andes virus," the Department of Health and Human Services says.

item.title: Principal who tackled gunman says he didn't initially know he was shot
item.description: Kirk Moore, the principal at Pauls Valley High School in Oklahoma, exclusively told CBS News how he acted on "just instinct" when tackling a school shooter.

item.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect Cole Allen pleads not guilty
item.description: Allen is charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump, assaulting a federal officer with a deadly weapon and two gun counts.

item.title: Trump says Iran's response to peace proposal "totally unacceptable"
item.description: President Trump didn't provide details on the issues he had with the response or what would come next.
